dial.summary: Net score holds at +2 (4 supportive: Global M2 ~$102.7T on an intact 4-week uptrend of ~$1.0T within the latest available May 2026 data, Fed balance sheet $6.74T flat with no aggressive QT, BTC funding rates near zero at +0.01%, stablecoin dominance 13.22% continuing its confirmed 30-day downtrend from ~14.3–14.5% in mid-June; 2 neutral: ISM Manufacturing 53.3% above 50 but falling 4-week with next July release August 3, 2s10s +0.43% positive but flat on a 30-day basis; 2 headwinds: DXY ~100.40 still above the 100 neutral-band ceiling though declining sharply post-CPI/PPI, real yields 2.33% elevated and rising on a 4-week basis); back-to-back disinflationary data—June CPI headline 3.5% YoY on July 14 and soft June PPI on July 15—have pushed DXY from 100.92 to ~100.40 and are opening a compression path for real yields, bringing the two remaining headwinds within striking distance of a flip; BTC.D firm at 56.5%, TOTAL crypto rising to $2.31T, and stablecoin dominance falling to 13.22% all confirm spot-driven capital rotation consistent with the BTC Accumulation regime.

indicators.name: Global Liquidity (Global M2)
indicators.note: MacroMicro 4-bank aggregate (Fed/ECB/BOJ/PBOC) at ~$102.7T through May 2026—the latest available data point given the 6–8 week reporting lag; June 2026 aggregate data not yet available; 4-week trend up within the May dataset: rose ~$1.0T from ~$101.7T (~30 days prior in the series) to ~$102.7T, a +~1.0% gain well above the 0.5% flat threshold; US M2 alone at $23.05T (May 2026, next BLS release July 28); Mises Institute notes money-supply growth in 2026 rose to a multi-year high as the Fed maintained its balance sheet; status supportive (4-week trend clearly up within available data, YoY growth positive); value unchanged this run—data lag, no June 2026 aggregate published yet.

indicators.name: ISM Manufacturing PMI
indicators.note: June 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI confirmed at 53.3%, released July 1—sixth consecutive month of expansion but 0.7pp below May's 54.0%; no July 2026 data—next ISM Manufacturing PMI for July releases August 3, 2026; 4-week trend down (May 54.0% → June 53.3%); new orders 56.0% (-0.8pp), production 52.2% (-2.1pp), employment 49.7% (still contracting), prices paid 73.0% (-9.1pp, largest single-month drop since July 2022, consistent with June CPI and June PPI disinflationary signal); status neutral (above 50 but falling—supportive requires both above 50 AND rising; headwind threshold requires below 48 and falling); value unchanged this run—no new ISM data available until August 3.

indicators.name: DXY (US Dollar Index)
indicators.note: ICE DXY at ~100.40 on July 15–16, 2026, following back-to-back soft inflation prints: June CPI (3.5% YoY vs 3.8–3.9% expected, July 14) drove DXY from 100.92 to 100.79, and soft June PPI (July 15) extended the decline approximately 0.5% toward the 100.40 area; 4-week trend flat—DXY was near ~100.30 post-June 16–17 FOMC (hawkish dot plot) and is now ~100.40, a change of only +0.10%, well within the 0.5% flat threshold (trend revised from 'up' now that the 4-week anchor is the post-FOMC June 16 level rather than the pre-FOMC June 11–12 level); status headwind (ICE DXY at ~100.40 remains above the 100 neutral-band ceiling; a sustained close below 100 is required to flip to neutral, and the post-PPI trajectory is the closest DXY has been to that threshold since early June).

indicators.name: Real Yields (US10Y - CPI)
indicators.note: 10-year TIPS real yield (DFII10) at 2.33% as of July 14, 2026 (latest confirmed FRED data, -0.01pp from July 13); July 15 soft PPI likely compressed real yields modestly (nominal 10Y fell ~6bps to ~4.57% on July 15 after PPI, breakevens may have moved in tandem), but confirmed July 15–16 DFII10 data not yet available; 4-week trend up—DFII10 has risen ~0.15–0.19pp from approximately 2.14–2.18% on June 16 (post-FOMC) to 2.33%, well above the 5bp flat threshold; back-to-back soft CPI + PPI prints open a compression path, but a sustained move below 2% is required to flip from headwind to neutral; status headwind (above 2% and rising on 4-week basis).

indicators.name: 2s10s Yield Curve
indicators.note: 10-year Treasury at ~4.57% and 2-year at ~4.14% as of July 15, 2026 (after soft June PPI: 10Y fell ~6bps, 2Y fell ~14bps on the day)—spread ~+0.43%; 4-week trend flat: spread was approximately +0.39% on June 16 (post-FOMC, rate held at 5.25–5.50% with hawkish dot plot), now +0.43%, a change of ~+4bps just within the 5bps flat threshold; the soft PPI on July 15 caused the 2-year to fall more sharply than the 10-year (bull steepener) but this intraday move does not yet constitute a sustained 30-day steepening trend; status neutral (positive spread well clear of the -50bps deep-inversion headwind threshold, but not yet sustaining a steepening trend across the full 30-day window—supportive requires positive AND steepening).

indicators.name: Fed Balance Sheet
indicators.note: H.4.1 balance sheet for week ended July 9, 2026 (released July 10): total assets $6,735,609M ($6.74T), up $11B from the prior week and ~$73B above year-ago levels; H.4.1 for week ended July 15 releases today July 16 at 4:30 PM ET (not yet available at time of this run); 4-week comparison: week ended ~June 11 estimated ~$6.71T; 4-week change ~+$25–30B / +0.4%, within the flat threshold; status supportive (balance sheet flat with no acceleration of QT, no aggressive reserve drain, YoY growth modest but positive).

indicators.name: BTC Funding Rates (perps avg)
indicators.note: BTC perpetual funding rate held near +0.01% per 8-hour interval through mid-July 2026 (Binance/Bybit/OKX aggregate via CoinGlass); specific July 16 real-time rate could not be sourced this run—value unchanged from prior run; context from market commentary confirms that into July, funding rates have remained near-flat while spot volumes carry the price move, which is a hallmark of healthy accumulation dynamics rather than overleveraged longs; BTC.D at 56.5% (firm, slightly up from 56.3%), total crypto market cap at $2.31T (rising from $2.26T); status supportive (well below the 0.04% overheating threshold; near-zero funding during TOTAL market cap expansion confirms spot-led bid).

indicators.name: Stablecoin Dominance
indicators.note: Stablecoin market cap ~$305B against total crypto market cap ~$2.31T as of mid-July 2026 (CoinGecko global charts: ~13.22%); down from 13.58% on July 14 and from ~14.3–14.5% in mid-June—the 30-day downtrend remains clearly intact indicating net capital rotation out of stablecoins and into the broader crypto ecosystem; BTC.D firm at 56.5% while stablecoin dominance trends lower 30-day is consistent with BTC accumulation rather than broad altcoin rotation; status supportive (30-day downtrend intact; headwind condition requires rising stablecoin dominance while TOTAL crypto market cap is also rising, which does not apply—both TOTAL rising and dominance falling, a bullish divergence).

upcoming.title: Housing Starts & Building Permits / Industrial Production / UMich Sentiment (June–July 2026)
upcoming.note: Census Bureau new residential construction for June at 8:30 AM ET—housing demand signal in a still-elevated rate environment (real yields 2.33%, 10Y ~4.57% after PPI sell-off); Federal Reserve Industrial Production & Capacity Utilization (June) at 9:15 AM ET—cross-checks the ISM Manufacturing expansion (53.3%, sixth consecutive month above 50); University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment preliminary (July) at 10:00 AM ET—the 1-year and 5-year inflation expectations components carry outsized weight for the Warsh Fed's reaction function, especially after the back-to-back soft CPI (3.5% YoY) and PPI prints; these are the last significant data releases before the FOMC blackout begins Saturday July 18 and runs through July 30.

upcoming.title: NAHB Housing Market Index (July 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Home Builders / Wells Fargo Housing Market Index for July 2026 at 10:00 AM ET—builder sentiment leading indicator; FOMC blackout runs July 18–30, so no Fed commentary accompanies this week's releases; the week culminates with the July 23 ECB rate decision and July 23 Initial Jobless Claims, followed by Flash PMIs on July 24.

upcoming.title: Existing Home Sales (June 2026)
upcoming.note: National Association of Realtors (NAR) Existing Home Sales for June 2026—closings on previously owned homes reflecting mortgage lock-in dynamics in a still-elevated rate environment (10Y ~4.57% after July 15 PPI sell-off, real yields 2.33%); released deep within the FOMC blackout (July 18–30) so no Fed amplification; one trading day before the ECB rate decision on July 23 which will set cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC.

upcoming.title: ECB Rate Decision (July 2026)
upcoming.note: ECB Governing Council decision at 13:45 CET (7:45 AM ET), press conference at 14:30 CET (8:30 AM ET)—July is a non-projection meeting (no updated Staff Economic Projections); ECB last acted June 11 with deposit rate at 2.25–2.65%; Lagarde's press conference tone is the primary market driver given the non-projection format; will assess whether global disinflation (US CPI 3.5% YoY July 14, soft US PPI July 15) and post-ceasefire commodity easing reduce the case for further tightening; ECB guidance sets cross-asset context for the July 28–29 FOMC; Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 19) also at 8:30 AM ET—last claims print before the FOMC rate decision July 29.

upcoming.title: Initial Jobless Claims (week ended July 19, 2026)
upcoming.note: Weekly UI claims at 8:30 AM ET—same morning as ECB decision; last jobless claims print before the July 28–29 FOMC rate decision (announced Wednesday July 29 at 2:00 PM ET); two consecutive weeks above 240K would materially shift rate-cut pricing entering the Wednesday announcement; the ECB + claims combination on July 23 is the most consequential macro morning before the FOMC meeting begins July 28.
